creator: markun@onohara.to
text:
I make a little bit change, based on NIIMI-san's port.
Tested on 5.5-RELEASE(i386) and 6.1-RELEASE(i386), and I can't test on
amd64.

- update to VMware Server 1.0.1 build 29996
- revert to former fetch and extract procedures
    because it requires agreement to EULA to download VMware Server.
    I think that to download VMware Server archive without
    EULA is inappropriate.



shell archive is as follows.


# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	vmware-server-guestd1
#	vmware-server-guestd1/Makefile
#	vmware-server-guestd1/distinfo
#	vmware-server-guestd1/files
#	vmware-server-guestd1/files/vmware-guestd.sh.in
#	vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr
#	vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr.tools
#	vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist
#	vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist.tools
#	vmware-server-tools1
#	vmware-server-tools1/Makefile
#
echo c - vmware-server-guestd1
mkdir -p vmware-server-guestd1 > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/Makefile << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	VMware tools for FreeBSD
X# Date created:		10 Aug 2000
X# Whom:			sa2c@sa2c.net
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	vmware
XPORTVERSION=	${VMWARE_VER}.${BUILD_VER}
XCATEGORIES=	emulators
XMASTER_SITES=	# bundled with VMware Server
XPKGNAMESUFFIX?=	-server-guestd
XDISTNAME=	vmware-freebsd-tools
X
XMAINTAINER=	sa2c@sa2c.net
XCOMMENT?=	VMware guest OS supporting daemon (VMware Server 1.x, FreeBSD version)
X
X.if defined(VMWARE_X_PORTS)
XRUN_DEPENDS=	${LOCALBASE}/sbin/vmware-guestd:${PORTSDIR}/emulators/vmware-server-guestd1
X.endif
X
XDISTDIR=	${MOUNT_PT}
XIGNOREFILES=	${DISTFILES}
XWRKSRC=		${WRKDIR}/vmware-tools-distrib
X
XONLY_FOR_ARCHS=	i386 amd64
XUSE_RC_SUBR=	vmware-guestd.sh
X.if defined(VMWARE_X_PORTS)
XUSE_X_PREFIX=	yes
XNO_BUILD=	yes
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_GTK)
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXGTKTOOL:=""
XUSE_ICONV=	yes
XUSE_GETTEXT=	yes
XUSE_XLIB=	yes
XUSE_GNOME=	glib12 gtk12
X.else
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXGTKTOOL:="@comment "
X.endif
X.endif
X
XRESTRICTED=	Not sure if we can redistribute this.
XNO_PACKAGE=	${RESTRICTED}
X
XVMWARE_VER=	1.0.1
XBUILD_VER=	29996
X
X.include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
X
X.if ${OSVERSION} < 500000
XMOUNT_DEV?=	/dev/acd0c
X.else
XMOUNT_DEV?=	/dev/acd0
X.endif
XMOUNT_PT?=	/mnt
XMOUNT=		/sbin/mount
XUMOUNT=		/sbin/umount
X
X.if ${ARCH} == i386
XBITS?=		32
X.else # ${ARCH} == amd64
XBITS?=		64
X.endif
X
X.if ${OSVERSION} < 600000
XSFX=		${BITS}
X.else
XSFX=		${BITS}-6
X.endif
X
X.if defined(VMWARE_X_PORTS)
X
X.if ${X_WINDOW_SYSTEM:L} == xfree86-3
X.if ${BITS} == 32
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER3:=""
X.else
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER3:="@comment "
X.endif
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER4:="@comment "
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXVMMOUSE:="@comment "
X.elif ${X_WINDOW_SYSTEM:L} == xfree86-4
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER3:="@comment "
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER4:=""
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXVMMOUSE:=""
X.else
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER3:="@comment "
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXSERVER4:="@comment "
XPLIST_SUB+=	INSTALLXVMMOUSE:=""
X.endif
X
X.else
X
XVMWARE_KMODDIR=	${PREFIX}/lib/vmware-tools/modules
X
X.if !defined(WITHOUT_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL) && exists(/usr/src/sys/Makefile)
XWITH_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL=YES
X.endif
X.if !defined(WITHOUT_VMWARE_VMXNET) && (${BITS} == 32)
XWITH_VMWARE_VMXNET=YES
X.endif
X
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL)
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMMEMCTL:=""
X.else
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMMEMCTL:="@comment "
X.endif
X
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMXNET) && (${BITS} == 32)
X.if (${OSVERSION} >= 480000) && (${OSVERSION} < 500000)
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMXNET:=""
XVMWARE_VMXNET_PATH=	4.9/vmxnet.ko
X.elif (${OSVERSION} >= 500000) && (${OSVERSION} < 600000)
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMXNET:=""
XVMWARE_VMXNET_PATH=	5.3-i386/vmxnet.ko
X.elif (${OSVERSION} >= 600000) && (${OSVERSION} < 700000)
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMXNET:=""
XVMWARE_VMXNET_PATH=	6.0-i386/vmxnet.ko
X.else
X# VMware doesn't provide vmxnet.ko for other versions
X.undef WITH_VMWARE_VMXNET
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMXNET:="@comment "
X.endif
X.else
X.undef WITH_VMWARE_VMXNET
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_VMXNET:="@comment "
X.endif
X
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL) || defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMXNET)
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_KMODDIR:=""
X.else
XPLIST_SUB+=	VMWARE_KMODDIR:="@comment "
X.endif
X
X.endif
X
Xfetch-list:
X	@${DO_NADA}
X
Xdo-fetch:
X	@${ECHO} ""
X	@${ECHO} "========================================================================"
X	@${ECHO} "Choose \"VM\" -> \"Install VMware Tools...\" from VMware Server Console"
X	@${ECHO} "menu to connect VM's CD-ROM drive and installation CD image temporary."
X	@${ECHO} "Press \"Install\" button when a dialog pops up."
X	@${ECHO} "========================================================================"
X	@${ECHO} ""
X.if !defined(BATCH)
X	@${ECHO} "This port mounts ${MOUNT_DEV} to ${MOUNT_PT}."
X	@${ECHO} ""
X	@${ECHO} -n "Are you ready? [Y/n]: "
X	@(read line;                    \
X	  case "$${line}" in            \
X		[Nn]*)                  \
X			${FALSE} ;;     \
X		*)                      \
X			${TRUE} ;;      \
X	  esac)
X.endif
X	${MKDIR} ${MOUNT_PT}
X	-${UMOUNT} ${MOUNT_PT} 2>&1 >/dev/null
X	-${UMOUNT} ${MOUNT_DEV} 2>&1 >/dev/null
X	${MOUNT} -t cd9660 ${MOUNT_DEV} ${MOUNT_PT}
X
Xpost-extract:
X	${UMOUNT} ${MOUNT_PT}
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL)
X	(cd ${WRKDIR}; ${TAR} xf ${WRKSRC}/lib/modules/source/vmmemctl.tar)
X.endif
X
X.if defined(VMWARE_X_PORTS)
X
Xdo-install:
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/lib/bin${SFX}/vmware-toolbox-tcl ${X11BASE}/bin
X	${LN} -sfh vmware-toolbox-tcl ${X11BASE}/bin/vmware-toolbox
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_GTK)
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/lib/bin${SFX}/vmware-toolbox-gtk ${X11BASE}/bin
X.endif
X.if ${X_WINDOW_SYSTEM:L} != xfree86-3
X	if [ ! -d ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/input ] ; then \
X		${MKDIR} ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/input ; \
X	fi
X.endif
X.if ${X_WINDOW_SYSTEM:L} == xfree86-3
X.if ${BITS} == 32
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-3/XF86_VMware_4.5 ${X11BASE}/bin/XF86_VMware
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-3/XF86Config ${X11BASE}/etc/XF86Config_VMware
X	@${ECHO} ""
X	@${ECHO} "You have installed XFree86 3.x X Server for VMware."
X	@${ECHO} "${X11BASE}/etc/XF86Config_VMware is a sample XF86Config file."
X	@${ECHO} "Copy this file to /etc/XF86Config before starting X."
X.else
X	@${ECHO} "There is no XFree86 3.x X server for your system."
X	@${ECHO} "Consider upgrading your X to XFree86 4.x or switch to X.Org."
X.endif
X.elif ${X_WINDOW_SYSTEM:L} == xfree86-4
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-4/XF86Config-4 ${X11BASE}/etc/XF86Config-4_VMware
X	if [ ! -d ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/drivers ] ; then \
X		${MKDIR} ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/drivers ; \
X	fi
X.if ${BITS} == 32
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-4/4.3.x/vmware_drv.o ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/drivers/vmware_drv.o_VMware
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-4/4.2.x/vmmouse_drv.o ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/input/vmmouse_drv.o_VMware
X.else
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-4/4.3.x_64/vmware_drv.o ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/drivers/vmware_drv.o_VMware
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XFree86-4/4.3.x_64/vmmouse_drv.o ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/input/vmmouse_drv.o_VMware
X.endif
X.else
X.if ${BITS} == 32
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XOrg/6.8.x/vmmouse_drv.o ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/input/vmmouse_drv.o_VMware
X.else
X	${INSTALL_DATA} ${WRKSRC}/lib/configurator/XOrg/6.8.x_64/vmmouse_drv.o ${X11BASE}/lib/modules/input/vmmouse_drv.o_VMware
X.endif
X.endif
X	-${WRKSRC}/lib/sbin${SFX}/vmware-guestd --cmd toolinstall.end
X
X.else
X
Xpost-patch:
X	${REINPLACE_CMD} 's|\0152\013\0350|\0152\\\n\0350|g' \
X		${WRKSRC}/lib/sbin${BITS}/vmware-checkvm
X
Xdo-build:
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL)
X	(cd ${WRKDIR}/vmmemctl-only; make)
X.endif
X
Xdo-install:
X	@if [ -f ${PREFIX}/etc/rc.d/${USE_RC_SUBR} ]; then \
X		${ECHO_CMD} "Remove old ${PREFIX}/etc/rc.d/${USE_RC_SUBR} before install."; \
X		exit 1; \
X	fi
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/lib/sbin${SFX}/vmware-guestd ${PREFIX}/sbin
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/lib/sbin${SFX}/vmware-checkvm ${PREFIX}/sbin
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMMEMCTL)
X	${MKDIR} ${VMWARE_KMODDIR}
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKDIR}/vmmemctl-only/vmmemctl.ko ${VMWARE_KMODDIR}
X.endif
X.if defined(WITH_VMWARE_VMXNET)
X	${MKDIR} ${VMWARE_KMODDIR}
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/lib/modules/binary/FreeBSD${VMWARE_VMXNET_PATH} ${VMWARE_KMODDIR}
X.endif
X	-${WRKSRC}/lib/sbin${SFX}/vmware-guestd --cmd toolinstall.end
X	${MKDIR} ${PREFIX}/share/vmware-tools
X	${LN} -sfh /usr/bin/true ${PREFIX}/share/vmware-tools/poweroff-vm-default
X	${LN} -sfh /usr/bin/true ${PREFIX}/share/vmware-tools/poweron-vm-default
X	${LN} -sfh /usr/bin/true ${PREFIX}/share/vmware-tools/resume-vm-default
X	${LN} -sfh /usr/bin/true ${PREFIX}/share/vmware-tools/suspend-vm-default
X	${LN} -sfh ${PREFIX}/share/vmware-tools /etc/vmware-tools
X
X.endif
X
X.include <bsd.port.post.mk>
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/Makefile
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/distinfo << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/distinfo'
XMD5 (vmware-freebsd-tools.tar.gz) = IGNORE
XSHA256 (vmware-freebsd-tools.tar.gz) = IGNORE
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/distinfo
echo c - vmware-server-guestd1/files
mkdir -p vmware-server-guestd1/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/files/vmware-guestd.sh.in
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/files/vmware-guestd.sh.in << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/files/vmware-guestd.sh.in'
X#!/bin/sh
X#
X# $FreeBSD: ports/emulators/vmware-guestd5/files/vmware-guestd.sh.in,v 1.9 2006/02/20 20:46:58 dougb Exp $
X#
X
X# PROVIDE: vmware-guestd
X# REQUIRE: DAEMON
X# BEFORE: LOGIN
X
XPREFIX=%%PREFIX%%
X. %%RC_SUBR%%
X
X# Global
Xcheckvm_cmd="${PREFIX}/sbin/vmware-checkvm > /dev/null"
X
X# Functions
Xvmware_guest_vmmemctl_start()
X{
X	echo 'Loading vmmemctl kernel module.'
X	kldload ${PREFIX}/lib/vmware-tools/modules/vmmemctl.ko >/dev/null 2>&1
X}
Xvmware_guest_vmxnet_start()
X{
X	echo 'Loading vmxnet kernel module.'
X	kldload ${PREFIX}/lib/vmware-tools/modules/vmxnet.ko >/dev/null 2>&1
X}
X
X# VMware kernel module: vmmemctl
Xname="vmware_guest_vmmemctl"
Xrcvar=`set_rcvar`
Xstart_precmd="${checkvm_cmd}"
Xstart_cmd="vmware_guest_vmmemctl_start"
Xstop_precmd="${checkvm_cmd}"
Xstop_cmd=":"
X
Xload_rc_config $name
X[ -z "$vmware_guest_vmmemctl_enable" ] && vmware_guest_vmmemctl_enable="NO"
X[ -n "$vmware_guest_kmod_enable" ] && vmware_guest_vmmemctl_enable="$vmware_guest_kmod_enable"
Xrun_rc_command "$1"
X
X# VMware kernel module: vmxnet
Xname="vmware_guest_vmxnet"
Xrcvar=`set_rcvar`
Xstart_precmd="${checkvm_cmd}"
Xstart_cmd="vmware_guest_vmxnet_start"
Xstop_precmd="${checkvm_cmd}"
Xstop_cmd=":"
X
Xload_rc_config $name
X[ -z "$vmware_guest_vmxnet_enable" ] && vmware_guest_vmxnet_enable="NO"
Xrun_rc_command "$1"
X
X# VMware guest daemon
Xname="vmware_guestd"
Xrcvar=`set_rcvar`
Xstart_precmd="${checkvm_cmd}"
Xunset start_cmd
Xstop_precmd="${checkvm_cmd}"
Xunset stop_cmd
Xcommand="${PREFIX}/sbin/vmware-guestd"
Xcommand_args="--halt-command '/sbin/shutdown -p now' >/dev/null 2>&1"
Xpidfile="/var/run/${name}.pid"
X
Xload_rc_config $name
X[ -z "$vmware_guestd_enable" ] && vmware_guestd_enable="YES"
X[ -z "$vmware_guestd_flags" ] && vmware_guestd_flags="--background ${pidfile}"
Xrun_rc_command "$1"
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/files/vmware-guestd.sh.in
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr'
Xvmware-guestd daemon is for time synchronization with host OS and others.
XBeware, this port is for FreeBSD _guest_ OS.  You don't need to install
Xthis ports to FreeBSD _host_ OS.  This ports also installs a kernel modules
Xfor FreeBSD guest.
X
XIf you want to run FreeBSD as a VMware guest OS, install this port
Xwould help you.  If you want to run X, install vmware-tools too.
X
XThis port assumes that a) users want only "must-have" binaries and
Xb) guest OS runs on a certain architecture and never be changed.  For
Xthose want full-feature of VMware Tools, use the installer bundled with
XVMware Tools instead.
X
XWWW:	http://www.vmware.com/products/server/
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr.tools
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr.tools << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr.tools'
XVMware tools support FreeBSD _guest_ OS running on VMware Server.
XBeware, this port is _not_ for FreeBSD _host_ OS.
X
XVMware tools for FreeBSD consist of vmware-toolbox which supports
Xseamless operation between the Host and the Guest OS, dual-boot
Xconfigulation files, and special XFree86 3.x server for Guest OS.
XHowever, we cannot install dual-boot configuration since it requires
Xto modify /etc files.
X
XThis ports will install vmware-toolbox to your computer, and
XXFree86 X server if you set XFREE86_VERSION to 3 in /etc/make.conf, 
XIf you already use XFree86 4.1.0 or later, you may use 'vmware'
Xdriver for X server.  Vmware-guestd is installed as a separate port,
Xports/emulators/vmware-server-guestd1, but this port install guestd
Xautomatically.
X
XThis port assumes that a) users want only "must-have" binaries and
Xb) guest OS runs on a certain architecture and never be changed.  For
Xthose want full-feature of VMware Tools, use the installer bundled with
XVMware Tools instead.
X
XWWW:	http://www.vmware.com/products/server/
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-descr.tools
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist'
Xsbin/vmware-checkvm
Xsbin/vmware-guestd
X%%VMWARE_VMMEMCTL:%%lib/vmware-tools/modules/vmmemctl.ko
X%%VMWARE_VMXNET:%%lib/vmware-tools/modules/vmxnet.ko
X%%VMWARE_KMODDIR:%%@dirrmtry lib/vmware-tools/modules
X%%VMWARE_KMODDIR:%%@dirrmtry lib/vmware-tools
Xshare/vmware-tools/poweroff-vm-default
Xshare/vmware-tools/poweron-vm-default
Xshare/vmware-tools/resume-vm-default
Xshare/vmware-tools/suspend-vm-default
X@dirrm share/vmware-tools
X@cwd /
Xetc/vmware-tools
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist
echo x - vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist.tools
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist.tools << 'END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist.tools'
Xbin/vmware-toolbox
Xbin/vmware-toolbox-tcl
X%%INSTALLXGTKTOOL:%%bin/vmware-toolbox-gtk
X%%INSTALLXSERVER3:%%bin/XF86_VMware
X%%INSTALLXSERVER3:%%etc/XF86Config_VMware
X%%INSTALLXSERVER4:%%etc/XF86Config-4_VMware
X%%INSTALLXSERVER4:%%lib/modules/drivers/vmware_drv.o_VMware
X%%INSTALLXVMMOUSE:%%lib/modules/input/vmmouse_drv.o_VMware
X%%INSTALLXSERVER4:%%@dirrmtry lib/modules/drivers
X%%INSTALLXVMMOUSE:%%@dirrmtry lib/modules/input
END-of-vmware-server-guestd1/pkg-plist.tools
echo c - vmware-server-tools1
mkdir -p vmware-server-tools1 > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - vmware-server-tools1/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >vmware-server-tools1/Makefile << 'END-of-vmware-server-tools1/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	VMware tools for FreeBSD
X# Date created:		10 Aug 2000
X# Whom:			sa2c@sa2c.net
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	vmware
XMASTER_SITES=	# bundled with VMware Server
XPKGNAMESUFFIX=	-server-tools
X
XCOMMENT=	VMware Tools for guest OS (VMware Server 1.x, FreeBSD version)
X
XMASTERDIR=	${.CURDIR}/../vmware-server-guestd1
XDESCR=		${PKGDIR}/pkg-descr.tools
XPLIST=		${PKGDIR}/pkg-plist.tools
X
XVMWARE_X_PORTS=	YES
X
X.include "${MASTERDIR}/Makefile"
END-of-vmware-server-tools1/Makefile
exit
